content.problemStatement:
--

**Write a program to Design a Nutrition analyzer**

- Create a class called "Food".
- Define the following instance variables (attributes) for the Food class with appropriate data types:
   - name (a String representing the name of the food item, e.g., "Apple" or "Burger")
   - calories (an integer representing the calorie count for the food item)
- Create a constructor for initializing the name and calories of the food
- Create a method named "display()" within the Food class that prints the name and calories of each food item.
- Inside the "main" method, instantiate objects of the Food class
   - A fruit object with name, calories content specified.
   - A fast food object with name, calories content specified.
- For each instantiated object, call the "display()" method to demonstrate Class and Method creation and showcase the nutrition information of the food item.

--
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
